---
label: "Continuous Compliance"
icon: "🔍"
---

## Continuous Compliance

AI governance is not a one-time audit -- it requires continuous monitoring, <JargonTip term="drift detection">drift detection</JargonTip>, and audit trails. Models degrade as data distributions shift.

<CodeBlock b64="" />

<ExpandableSection title="Audit Trail Requirements" icon="🔍" defaultOpen={true}>

1. **Decision logs**: Record every AI decision with inputs, outputs, model version, and timestamp.

2. **Model versioning**: Track every model change -- training data, hyperparameters, code, and performance.

3. **Incident response**: Document all fairness violations, drift events, and corrective actions.

</ExpandableSection>

<Quiz question="What is data drift?" options={["A database migration","When real-world data distribution changes from training data","Moving data to cloud","A backup type"]} correctIndex={1} explanation="Data drift occurs when statistical properties of real-world data change from the training distribution, causing performance to degrade silently." />
